vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: CFRP box beam center fuselage
Avionics bay
Wing spar socket
Front GPS boom mount
Belly camera window

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilt axis with bearing seats, servo linkage, rotation clearance arc

physics_paths.path: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings
Heat from motor and ESC dissipates through CFRP shell to ambient air
Nacelle rotates 0-90deg around tilt axis, driven by servo through worm gear
Power and control signals pass through harnesses to motor and ESC
Access covers provide service paths for motor and servo replacement

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual and tactile inspection during and post-manufacture

generated_parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
generated_parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

requirements: Torque from the tilt servo is transferred through the tilt axis to the bearing seat, which is anchored to the wing spar.
The tilt axis allows the nacelle to rotate from 0 to 93 degrees.
Servo maintenance and replacement is facilitated through the servo access port.

risk.desc: Potential mass overestimation due to conservative material properties.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for nacelle rotation.
risk.mit: Increase clearance clearance_mm to ensure smooth rotation.

risk.mit: Ensure servo access port is large enough for module replacement.

risk.mit: Verify and reinforce locking mechanisms with additional washers or nuts.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter tolerance controls during manufacturing.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilt axis with bearing seats, servo linkage, rotation clearance arc

physics_paths.description: Torque from the tilt servo is transferred through the tilt axis to the bearing seat, which is anchored to the wing spar.
The tilt axis allows the nacelle to rotate from 0 to 93 degrees.
Servo maintenance and replacement is facilitated through the servo access port.

integration_opportunities.description: The tilt axis integrates with the nacelle motor pod to enable tilting functionality.
The bearing seat integrates with the wing spar to provide structural support.
The servo linkage integrates with the servo mount to transfer torque.

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and CMM
